risk_if_not_implemented: Without Automated Reporting, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Incident Response Plan (IRP)
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to maintain and make available a current and viable Incident Response Plan (IRP) to all stakeholders.
compensating_control_1.justification: Incident Response Plan (IRP) (IRO-04) provides incident response capability that compensates for the absence of Automated Reporting (IRO-10.1) by enabling timely detection, containment, and recovery from security events in the absence of the primary control. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Contacts With Authorities
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to identify and document appropriate contacts with relevant law enforcement and regulatory bodies.
compensating_control_2.justification: Contacts With Authorities (GOV-06) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Automated Reporting (IRO-10.1) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
